name: Frontend, React, and Browser
description: Frontend architecture, React behaviour, state management, browser rendering, accessibility, performance, and migration.

topics.name: Feature-Based Frontend Architecture
topics.description: Organizing frontend code around product capabilities instead of file types.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Feature-based architecture keeps one capability’s components, hooks, API calls, state, tests, and types together. Routes mainly compose features.

## What problem does it solve?

Global components/hooks/services folders scatter one feature across the codebase and make ownership unclear.

## How it works

- Keep feature-specific code together.
- Use app for setup, pages for entry points, features for capabilities, and shared only for generic code.
- Give each feature a small public API.
- Define allowed import direction.

## Common mistakes

- Moving everything into shared too early.
- Importing another feature’s internals.
- Putting business workflows in route components.

## Key terms

- feature boundary — code owned by one capability
- entry point — route composing features
- public API — supported feature exports

hu.name: Feature-Based Frontend Architecture
hu.description: A frontend code product capabilityk köré szerveződik, nem file type szerint.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Egy product capability componentjei, hookjai, API calljai, state-je, testjei és type-jai együtt maradnak. A route főleg feature-öket compose-ol.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A globális components/hooks/services folder szétszórja ugyanazt a feature-t, ezért az ownership nehezen követhető.

## Hogyan működik?

- A feature-specifikus code maradjon együtt.
- Az app tartsa a setupot, a pages az entry pointokat, a features a capabilityket, a shared pedig csak a generic code-ot.
- A feature kis public API-t exportáljon.
- Legyen import direction.

## Gyakori hibák

- Mindent túl korán sharedbe tenni.
- Feature internalokat keresztbe importálni.
- Business workflow-t route componentben tartani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- feature boundary — egy capabilityhez tartozó code határa
- entry point — feature-öket összerakó route
- public API — támogatott feature export

topics.name: Reusable Components and Design Systems
topics.description: Creating shared UI from proven patterns, with tokens, documentation, and accessibility.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A reusable component represents a repeated UI pattern with a small stable API. A design system adds tokens, accessibility rules, examples, ownership, and governance.

## What problem does it solve?

Duplication creates inconsistency, but premature abstraction produces components with many flags and hidden product assumptions.

## How it works

- Generalize after real repeated use.
- Prefer composition and slots over many boolean props.
- Separate generic UI from business orchestration.
- Document loading, disabled, error, focus, and empty states.
- Version breaking changes.

## Common mistakes

- Building a universal component too early.
- Treating a design system as only a component library.
- Documenting only the happy path.

## Key terms

- design token — named reusable design value
- variant — supported component form
- Storybook story — isolated component example

hu.name: Reusable Components és Design System
hu.description: Valódi ismétlődő UI patternből készülő shared component tokennel, dokumentációval és accessibilityvel.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A reusable component ismétlődő UI patternt képvisel kis, stabil API-val. A design system ehhez tokeneket, accessibility szabályt, example-t, ownershipot és governance-t ad.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A duplication inkonzisztenciát okoz, a premature abstraction viszont sok flaggel és hidden assumptionnel terheli a componentet.

## Hogyan működik?

- Valós repeated use után generalizálj.
- Sok boolean prop helyett compositiont és slotot használj.
- A generic UI különüljön el a business orchestrationtől.
- Loading, disabled, error, focus és empty state legyen dokumentálva.
- Breaking change kapjon versiont.

## Gyakori hibák

- Universal componentet túl korán építeni.
- A design systemet csak component librarynek tekinteni.
- Csak happy pathot dokumentálni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- design token — reusable design value
- variant — támogatott component forma
- Storybook story — isolated component example

topics.name: From Figma to Production UI
topics.description: Turning visual designs into complete responsive, accessible product behaviour.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Implementing Figma means translating visual intent into complete behaviour, not copying pixels. Missing states, data contracts, responsiveness, keyboard interaction, and localization must be resolved.

## What problem does it solve?

Designs often show only the ideal desktop state, while production must handle errors, permissions, long text, and small screens.

## How it works

- Reuse existing design-system patterns.
- Clarify loading, empty, error, disabled, permission, and partial-data states.
- Check long text, localization, overflow, and responsive behaviour.
- Define keyboard, focus, hover, and screen-reader behaviour.
- Review with realistic content.

## Common mistakes

- Building only the screenshot’s happy path.
- Assuming fixed text length and width.
- Asking design questions only at sprint end.

## Key terms

- design intent — behaviour behind the mock-up
- missing state — required state absent from the design
- realistic content — production-like text and data

hu.name: Figma designből Production UI
hu.description: A visual design teljes responsive és accessible product behaviourré alakítása.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Figma implementáció nem pixelmásolás. A visual intent mellé missing state, data contract, responsive behaviour, keyboard interaction és localization is kell.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A design gyakran csak az ideális desktop state-et mutatja, productionben viszont error, permission, long text és small screen is van.

## Hogyan működik?

- Keresd a meglévő design-system patternt.
- Tisztázd a loading, empty, error, disabled, permission és partial-data state-et.
- Vizsgáld a long textet, localizationt, overflow-t és responsive behaviourt.
- Definiáld a keyboard, focus, hover és screen-reader behaviourt.
- Realistic contenttel reviewzz.

## Gyakori hibák

- Csak a screenshot happy pathját megépíteni.
- Fix text lengthre és widthre számítani.
- A sprint végére hagyni a design kérdéseket.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- design intent — a mock-up mögötti behaviour
- missing state — a designból hiányzó state
- realistic content — productionhöz hasonló content

topics.name: Modular Frontend and Microfrontends
topics.description: Separating features in one application or across independently deployed frontend parts.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A modular frontend has strong feature boundaries but one runtime and deployment. Microfrontends add independent build and deployment, then compose the parts into one experience.

## What problem does it solve?

Independent teams may need deployment autonomy, but microfrontends add runtime integration, dependency, routing, auth, observability, and UX costs.

## How it works

- Start with modular boundaries unless independent deployment is a real requirement.
- Keep communication small and contract-based.
- Share a design system and navigation conventions.
- Define ownership for auth, routing, analytics, errors, and dependencies.

## Common mistakes

- Calling every feature folder a microfrontend.
- Choosing microfrontends for a small team.
- Sharing one giant global store.

## Key terms

- modular frontend — features in one runtime/deployment
- microfrontend — independently deployable capability
- shell/host — app composing the parts

hu.name: Modular Frontend és Microfrontends
hu.description: Feature-ek szétválasztása egy appon belül vagy independently deployed részek között.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A modular frontend strong boundaryt használ egy runtime és deployment mellett. A microfrontend independent buildet és deploymentet ad, majd a részeket egy experience-be compose-olja.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Team autonomyhoz hasznos lehet, de runtime integration, dependency, routing, auth, observability és UX costot hoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Modular boundary legyen az alap, amíg nincs valós independent deployment need.
- A communication legyen kevés és contract-based.
- Kell közös design system és navigation convention.
- Auth, routing, analytics, error és dependency ownership legyen tiszta.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden feature foldert microfrontendnek nevezni.
- Kis teamnek microfrontendet választani.
- Egy giant global store-t megosztani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- modular frontend — feature-ek egy runtime-ban/deploymentben
- microfrontend — independently deployable capability
- shell/host — a részeket compositionáló app

topics.name: React Rendering Model
topics.description: Render, reconciliation, commit, and the difference between component execution and DOM updates.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

React calls component functions to create the next UI description, reconciles it with the previous result, and commits only required host changes.

## What problem does it solve?

Confusing a component render with a DOM update leads to mutation bugs, render side effects, and premature optimization.

## How it works

- State updates, parent renders, Context changes, and store subscriptions can trigger rendering.
- Render must be pure.
- Reconciliation matches old and new elements.
- Commit applies DOM changes and effects run afterward.
- A component re-run does not guarantee a DOM change.

## Common mistakes

- Network calls during render.
- Mutating state and expecting React to detect it.
- Assuming every re-render is expensive.

## Key terms

- render phase — computes the next UI
- reconciliation — matches element identity
- commit phase — applies host changes

hu.name: React Rendering Model
hu.description: A render, reconciliation és commit működése, valamint a component execution és DOM update különbsége.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

React component functionöket futtat a következő UI descriptionhöz, ezt reconciliation során összeveti az előzővel, majd csak a szükséges host change-et commitolja.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A component render és DOM update összekeverése mutation bugot, render side effectet és premature optimizationt okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- State update, parent render, Context change és store subscription triggerelhet renderelést.
- A render pure legyen.
- A reconciliation régi és új elementet párosít.
- A commit alkalmazza a DOM change-et, majd effect fut.
- Component újrafutása nem jelent biztos DOM change-et.

## Gyakori hibák

- Network callt render közben indítani.
- State-et mutálni és React detectálását várni.
- Minden re-rendert drágának tekinteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- render phase — a következő UI kiszámítása
- reconciliation — element identity párosítása
- commit phase — host change alkalmazása

topics.name: Reconciliation and List Keys
topics.description: Preserving component identity across list insertion, deletion, and reordering.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

React uses element type and sibling keys to decide whether an existing component instance still represents the same logical item.

## What problem does it solve?

Index or random keys can move local state and focus to the wrong row or remount every item.

## How it works

- Use a stable identifier from the data.
- Keys need to be unique among siblings.
- Changing a key intentionally resets component state.
- Index keys are safe only for static lists.

## Common mistakes

- Using indexes for reorderable lists.
- Generating random keys during render.
- Expecting key as a normal child prop.

## Key terms

- key — stable sibling identity
- remount — replacing a component instance
- positional identity — identity based on position

hu.name: Reconciliation és List Keys
hu.description: Component identity megőrzése insertion, deletion és reorder közben.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

React az element type és sibling key alapján dönti el, hogy a component instance ugyanazt a logikai itemet képviseli-e.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Index vagy random key local state-et és focus-t vihet rossz sorra, vagy minden itemet remountolhat.

## Hogyan működik?

- A data stabil identifierét használd.
- A key siblingek között legyen unique.
- Key cseréje szándékosan resetelheti a state-et.
- Index key csak static listánál safe.

## Gyakori hibák

- Reorderelhető listán indexet használni.
- Render közben random key-t generálni.
- Azt várni, hogy a child normál propként megkapja a key-t.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- key — stabil sibling identity
- remount — component instance lecserélése
- positional identity — pozícióból számított identity

topics.name: State Ownership and Placement
topics.description: Choosing the correct home for local, shared, server, and navigation state.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

State ownership identifies which component or subsystem is responsible for a value. Local UI, shared client, server, and navigation state have different lifecycles.

## What problem does it solve?

Putting everything in one global store increases coupling; keeping shared data too local creates duplicated sources of truth.

## How it works

- Keep state close to the UI that uses it.
- Lift only to the nearest common owner.
- Use Context for stable subtree-wide values.
- Use a query cache for remote data.
- Put shareable filters and pagination in the URL.
- Use a store only for complex shared client state.

## Common mistakes

- Storing every modal in Redux.
- Copying server data into several states.
- Keeping shareable navigation state only in memory.

## Key terms

- source of truth — authoritative owner
- server state — remote data with freshness concerns
- navigation state — state represented by the URL

hu.name: State Ownership és Elhelyezés
hu.description: Local, shared, server és navigation state megfelelő helyének kiválasztása.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A state ownership megmondja, mely component vagy subsystem felel egy value-ért. A local UI, shared client, server és navigation state lifecycle-ja eltér.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Ha minden global store-ba kerül, nő a coupling; ha shared data túl local marad, duplicated source of truth keletkezik.

## Hogyan működik?

- A state maradjon közel a használó UI-hoz.
- Csak a nearest common ownerhez lifteld.
- Stabil subtree-wide value-hoz Context jó.
- Remote data query cache-be való.
- Shareable filter és pagination kerüljön URL-be.
- Store csak complex shared client state esetén kell.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden modalt Reduxban tárolni.
- Server data-t több state-be bemásolni.
- Shareable navigation state-et csak memoryban tartani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- source of truth — authoritative owner
- server state — freshness problémát hordozó remote data
- navigation state — URL-ben reprezentált state

topics.name: Context and useReducer
topics.description: Providing subtree-wide values with predictable local transitions.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Context provides values to a subtree without forwarding through every intermediate component. useReducer adds explicit action-based transitions.

## What problem does it solve?

It removes noisy prop drilling, but one giant frequently changing Context can re-render most consumers.

## How it works

- Use composition when it naturally removes forwarding.
- Split contexts by domain and update frequency.
- Separate state and dispatch contexts where useful.
- Keep reducers pure and actions domain-oriented.
- Move to a store when selectors, middleware, or cross-module workflows justify it.

## Common mistakes

- Putting the whole app into one Context.
- Recreating provider objects unnecessarily.
- Using Context for high-frequency global values.

## Key terms

- provider — component supplying Context
- consumer — component reading Context
- reducer — pure transition function

hu.name: Context és useReducer
hu.description: Subtree-wide value továbbítása predictable local transitionökkel.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Context value-t ad egy subtree-nek intermediate prop forwarding nélkül. A useReducer explicit action-based transitiont ad.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Csökkenti a prop drillingot, de egy giant, gyakran változó Context sok consumert re-renderelhet.

## Hogyan működik?

- Compositiont használj, ha természetesen megszünteti a forwardingot.
- Contextet domain és update frequency szerint bonts.
- Szükség esetén state és dispatch Context legyen külön.
- A reducer pure, az action domain-oriented legyen.
- Selector, middleware vagy cross-module workflow esetén store lehet jobb.

## Gyakori hibák

- Az egész appot egy Contextbe tenni.
- Provider objectet feleslegesen újragyártani.
- High-frequency global value-t Contextben tartani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- provider — Contextet adó component
- consumer — Contextet olvasó component
- reducer — pure transition function

topics.name: Redux, Reducers, and Selectors
topics.description: Predictable shared client state with explicit actions, pure transitions, and derived data.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Redux stores selected shared client state. Components dispatch domain actions, reducers produce the next state, and selectors hide store shape and derive UI data.

## What problem does it solve?

Large modular applications may need several independent areas to react to the same event while remaining traceable.

## How it works

- Store only genuinely shared client state.
- Keep reducers pure.
- Use domain actions rather than arbitrary setters.
- Normalize shared entities.
- Hide store shape behind selectors.
- Keep server caching in a query tool unless a real Redux need exists.

## Common mistakes

- Putting every UI value in Redux.
- Side effects in reducers.
- Selectors returning fresh objects on every call.
- Duplicating entities across slices.

## Key terms

- action — description of what happened
- reducer — pure next-state function
- selector — reads or derives store data
- normalized state — entities stored once by ID

hu.name: Redux, Reducers és Selectors
hu.description: Predictable shared client state explicit actionnel, pure transitionnel és derived data-val.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Redux kiválasztott shared client state-et tart. A component domain actiont dispatchol, a reducer next state-et készít, a selector pedig elrejti a store shape-et és derivál.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Nagy modular appban több independent rész reagálhat ugyanarra az eventre, miközben traceability kell.

## Hogyan működik?

- Csak valóban shared client state kerüljön Reduxba.
- A reducer pure legyen.
- Arbitrary setter helyett domain actiont használj.
- Shared entity legyen normalized.
- A store shape selector mögé kerüljön.
- Server cache alapból query tool feladata.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden UI value-t Reduxban tartani.
- Side effectet reducerbe tenni.
- Minden callnál fresh objectet adó selectort írni.
- Entityt több slice-ban duplikálni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- action — leírja, mi történt
- reducer — pure next-state function
- selector — store data-t olvas vagy derivál
- normalized state — entity egyszer, ID alapján tárolva

topics.name: Redux-Saga and Async Workflows
topics.description: Coordinating complex asynchronous workflows with generator-based effects.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Redux-Saga listens to actions and coordinates async workflows through generator effects such as call, put, take, race, and cancellation.

## What problem does it solve?

Cross-module sequencing, cancellation, retries, and background work can be difficult to express as isolated component effects.

## How it works

- Use Saga for orchestration, not routine fetching.
- Keep yielded effects declarative and testable.
- Use takeLatest when obsolete work should be cancelled.
- Model timeout, cancellation, and error paths.
- Prefer a query library when the main need is cache and invalidation.

## Common mistakes

- Using Saga for every API call.
- Spreading business rules across many watchers.
- Forgetting stale-response and cancellation behaviour.

## Key terms

- watcher — saga listening for actions
- worker — saga executing a workflow
- effect — declarative yielded instruction
- cancellation — stopping obsolete work

hu.name: Redux-Saga és Async Workflows
hu.description: Complex asynchronous workflow-k koordinálása generator-based effectekkel.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Redux-Saga actionöket figyel, és generator effectekkel, például call, put, take, race és cancellation segítségével koordinál workflow-kat.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Cross-module sequencing, cancellation, retry és background work nehezen fér el isolated component effectekben.

## Hogyan működik?

- Saga orchestrationre való, nem routine fetchre.
- A yielded effect declarative és tesztelhető legyen.
- takeLatest akkor jó, ha obsolete work cancelölhető.
- Timeout, cancellation és error path legyen explicit.
- Cache és invalidation problémára query library jobb.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden API callhoz Sagát használni.
- Business rule-t sok watcher között szétszórni.
- Stale response-t és cancellationt elfelejteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- watcher — actiont figyelő saga
- worker — workflow-t futtató saga
- effect — declarative yielded instruction
- cancellation — obsolete work leállítása

topics.name: Hooks and Effects
topics.description: Composing stateful behaviour and synchronizing React with external systems.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Hooks compose stateful behaviour in function components. Effects synchronize with systems React does not own, such as subscriptions, timers, network requests, DOM APIs, or third-party libraries.

## What problem does it solve?

Conditional Hook calls break state association, while effects used as general control flow create loops, stale closures, races, and duplicated state.

## How it works

- Call Hooks only at the top level of components or custom Hooks.
- Keep Hook order stable.
- Include every reactive effect dependency.
- Return cleanup for subscriptions, timers, listeners, and obsolete work.
- Derive values during render instead of syncing duplicates.
- Make setup safe under repeated mount-cleanup-mount checks.

## Common mistakes

- Calling Hooks conditionally.
- Omitting dependencies to force fewer runs.
- Starting subscriptions without cleanup.
- Copying props into state through an effect without need.

## Key terms

- custom Hook — reusable Hook composition
- dependency array — reactive effect inputs
- cleanup — undo previous synchronization
- stale closure — callback reading old logical state

hu.name: Hooks és Effects
hu.description: Stateful behaviour composition és Reacten kívüli rendszerekkel való synchronization.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Hook stateful behaviourt compose-ol function componentben. Az effect olyan rendszerrel szinkronizál, amelyet React nem ownol, például subscription, timer, network request, DOM API vagy third-party library.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Conditional Hook call eltöri a state associationt, a general control flow-ként használt effect pedig loopot, stale closure-t, race-t és duplicated state-et okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Hook csak component vagy custom Hook top leveljén fusson.
- A Hook order legyen stabil.
- Minden reactive effect dependency szerepeljen.
- Subscription, timer, listener és obsolete work kapjon cleanupot.
- Derivable value render közben készüljön.
- Setup legyen safe repeated mount-cleanup-mount alatt.

## Gyakori hibák

- Hookot conditionben hívni.
- Dependencyt kihagyni, hogy ritkábban fusson.
- Subscriptiont cleanup nélkül indítani.
- Propsot indok nélkül effecttel state-be másolni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- custom Hook — reusable Hook composition
- dependency array — reactive effect input
- cleanup — korábbi synchronization visszavonása
- stale closure — régi logical state-et olvasó callback

topics.name: React Memoization and Profiling
topics.description: Measuring render cost and applying memoization or virtualization deliberately.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

React memoization can skip a component render or reuse a calculation when inputs and references remain equal. Profiling identifies whether rendering is a meaningful bottleneck.

## What problem does it solve?

Memoizing everything adds complexity and often fails when parents create new object or function references each render.

## How it works

- Measure with React Profiler and browser tools first.
- Reduce state scope and split components before adding caches.
- Use memo for expensive children with usually equal props.
- Use useMemo for expensive calculations and useCallback when function identity matters.
- Treat caches as performance hints.
- Virtualize very large lists.

## Common mistakes

- Memoizing every value and function.
- Omitting dependencies to preserve identity.
- Passing fresh objects to memoized children.
- Using useMemo for correctness or side effects.

## Key terms

- stable reference — same identity across renders
- memo — component render optimization
- profiling — measurement of render work
- virtualization — mounting only visible rows

hu.name: React Memoization és Profiling
hu.description: Render cost mérése, majd targeted memoization vagy virtualization.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A React memoization kihagyhat component rendert vagy újrahasználhat calculationt, ha input és reference unchanged. A profiling mutatja meg, hogy a render valóban bottleneck-e.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Mindenhol memoization complexityt hoz, és nem segít, ha a parent minden renderben új object vagy function reference-et ad.

## Hogyan működik?

- Először React Profilerrel és browser toollal mérj.
- Cache előtt csökkentsd a state scope-ot és bontsd a componentet.
- memo expensive childhoz jó equal props mellett.
- useMemo expensive calculationhoz, useCallback fontos function identityhez kell.
- A cache performance hint.
- Nagy listát virtualizálj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden value-t és functiont memoizálni.
- Identityért dependencyt kihagyni.
- Memoized childnak fresh objectet adni.
- useMemo-t correctnessre vagy side effectre használni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- stable reference — ugyanaz az identity renderek között
- memo — component render optimization
- profiling — render work mérése
- virtualization — csak visible row mountolása

topics.name: Controlled and Uncontrolled Forms
topics.description: Choosing whether React state or the DOM owns input values and validation flow.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A controlled input gets its value from React state. An uncontrolled input keeps its value in the DOM and is read through a ref or submission.

## What problem does it solve?

Forms need validation, error association, reset, browser behaviour, and performance. Mixing ownership causes warnings and inconsistent values.

## How it works

- Use controlled fields when UI behaviour depends on every change.
- Use uncontrolled fields for simpler forms or suitable form libraries.
- Keep one owner per field.
- Preserve input after recoverable errors.
- Associate labels and field errors.
- Model submitting, success, and failure explicitly.

## Common mistakes

- Switching unexpectedly between controlled and uncontrolled.
- Clearing input after a server error.
- Showing one generic error without field association.

## Key terms

- controlled input — React owns the value
- uncontrolled input — DOM owns the value
- field error — validation tied to one input

hu.name: Controlled és Uncontrolled Forms
hu.description: Annak kiválasztása, hogy React state vagy a DOM ownolja az input value-t.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Controlled input value-ja React state-ből jön. Uncontrolled input az aktuális value-t a DOM-ban tartja, és ref vagy submission segítségével olvasható.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A form validationt, error associationt, resetet, browser behaviourt és performance-et kezel. Ownership keverése warningot és inkonzisztens value-t okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Controlled field jó, ha minden change befolyásolja a UI-t.
- Uncontrolled field egyszerű formnál vagy megfelelő form librarynél jó lehet.
- Egy fieldnek egy ownerje legyen.
- Recoverable error után maradjon meg az input.
- Label és field error kapcsolódjon.
- Submitting, success és failure legyen explicit.

## Gyakori hibák

- Controlled és uncontrolled state között váratlanul váltani.
- Server error után inputot törölni.
- Egy generic errort mutatni field association nélkül.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- controlled input — React ownolja a value-t
- uncontrolled input — DOM ownolja a value-t
- field error — egy inputhoz kötött validation

topics.name: Server State and Query Caches
topics.description: Managing remote data with freshness, caching, refetching, and invalidation.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Server state is owned by a remote system. Query libraries manage caching, request deduplication, stale data, refetching, retries, and invalidation.

## What problem does it solve?

Treating API responses as ordinary client state forces teams to rebuild freshness, races, and cache invalidation manually.

## How it works

- Identify queries with stable keys including relevant parameters.
- Define stale time and cache lifetime.
- Invalidate or update affected queries after mutations.
- Keep transient UI state outside the query cache.
- Distinguish initial loading from background refetching.
- Do not retry permanent permission or validation errors.

## Common mistakes

- Copying query data into local state without need.
- Reusing one key for different parameters.
- Treating cached data as permanently authoritative.

## Key terms

- query key — cache identifier
- stale data — cached data eligible for refetch
- invalidation — marking cached data outdated
- background refetch — refresh while old data remains visible

hu.name: Server State és Query Cache
hu.description: Remote data kezelése freshness, cache, refetch és invalidation segítségével.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A server state remote rendszer által owned data. A query library cache-t, request deduplicationt, stale data-t, refetch-et, retryt és invalidationt kezel.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Ha API response ordinary client state-ként van kezelve, a team kézzel építi újra a freshness, race és cache invalidation mechanizmust.

## Hogyan működik?

- Query stable keyt kapjon minden releváns parameterrel.
- Stale time és cache lifetime legyen definiálva.
- Mutation után az érintett query invalidate vagy update történjen.
- Transient UI state ne kerüljön query cache-be.
- Initial loading és background refetch legyen külön.
- Permission és validation errort ne retryolj automatikusan.

## Gyakori hibák

- Query data-t indok nélkül local state-be másolni.
- Külön parameterhez ugyanazt a key-t használni.
- Cached data-t örökké authoritative-nak tekinteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- query key — cache identifier
- stale data — refetchre jogosult cached data
- invalidation — cache outdatedként jelölése
- background refetch — refresh régi data mellett

topics.name: Async UI States and Optimistic Updates
topics.description: Designing loading, error, empty, success, pending, and rollback behaviour.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Async UI needs intentional loading, error, empty, success, and sometimes optimistic pending states. Optimistic UI shows an expected result before server confirmation and reconciles later.

## What problem does it solve?

Blank screens, generic errors, stale responses, and dishonest success make the app feel unreliable.

## How it works

- Use skeletons when preserving layout helps.
- Distinguish validation, permission, not-found, network, and server failures.
- Treat empty as guidance, not an error.
- Prevent old responses from overwriting new results.
- Use optimistic updates only for high-success, low-risk, reversible actions.
- Roll back or expose retry on failure.

## Common mistakes

- Building only the happy path.
- Showing one error for every failure.
- Treating empty as failure.
- Pretending payment succeeded before confirmation.

## Key terms

- skeleton — loading placeholder preserving layout
- empty state — no-data guidance
- optimistic update — local change before confirmation
- rollback — undo failed optimistic state

hu.name: Async UI State és Optimistic Update
hu.description: Loading, error, empty, success, pending és rollback behaviour tervezése.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Async UI-hoz intentional loading, error, empty, success és néha optimistic pending state kell. Optimistic UI server confirmation előtt mutatja a várható resultot, majd reconcile-ol.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Blank screen, generic error, stale response és dishonest success miatt az app unreliable-nak tűnik.

## Hogyan működik?

- Skeleton akkor jó, ha a layout helyét meg kell tartani.
- Validation, permission, not-found, network és server failure különüljön el.
- Az empty guidance, nem error.
- Old response ne írja felül az új resultot.
- Optimistic update csak high-success, low-risk, reversible actionnél legyen.
- Failure esetén rollback vagy retry kell.

## Gyakori hibák

- Csak happy pathot építeni.
- Minden failure-re ugyanazt az errort adni.
- Empty state-et failure-nek kezelni.
- Paymentet confirmation előtt sikeresnek mutatni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- skeleton — layoutot tartó loading placeholder
- empty state — no-data guidance
- optimistic update — local change confirmation előtt
- rollback — failed optimistic state visszavonása

topics.name: CSR, SSR, and Hydration
topics.description: Choosing where initial UI is rendered and keeping server and client output consistent.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

CSR renders the main UI in the browser. SSR produces HTML on the server. Hydration attaches React behaviour to server-rendered HTML. A mismatch occurs when the first client output differs.

## What problem does it solve?

CSR can delay initial content; SSR adds server cost, serialization boundaries, and deterministic-rendering requirements.

## How it works

- Choose by SEO, first-load, personalization, and infrastructure needs.
- Keep server and first client render deterministic.
- Use the same locale and initial data.
- Move browser-only behaviour to effects or client-only components.
- Distinguish visible HTML from fully interactive UI.
- Suppress mismatch warnings only for known intentional cases.

## Common mistakes

- Using window or localStorage during server render.
- Rendering time or random values differently.
- Assuming SSR is always faster.
- Hiding every mismatch warning.

## Key terms

- CSR — client-side rendering
- SSR — server-side rendering
- hydration — attaching client behaviour
- hydration mismatch — server/client output differs

hu.name: CSR, SSR és Hydration
hu.description: Initial UI render helyének kiválasztása és server-client output konzisztencia.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

CSR a main UI-t browserben rendereli. SSR serveren készít HTML-t. Hydration a React behaviourt hozzákapcsolja a server HTML-hez. Mismatch akkor van, ha a first client output eltér.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

CSR késleltetheti az initial contentet; SSR server costot, serialization boundaryt és deterministic-rendering requirementet hoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- SEO, first-load, personalization és infrastructure alapján válassz.
- A server és first client render legyen deterministic.
- Ugyanazt a locale-t és initial data-t használd.
- Browser-only behaviour effectbe vagy client-only componentbe kerüljön.
- A visible HTML és fully interactive UI nem ugyanaz.
- Warning suppression csak ismert intentional esetben legyen.

## Gyakori hibák

- Windowt vagy localStorage-t server render alatt használni.
- Time vagy random value-t eltérően renderelni.
- SSR-t mindig gyorsabbnak gondolni.
- Minden mismatch warningot elrejteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- CSR — client-side rendering
- SSR — server-side rendering
- hydration — client behaviour kapcsolása
- hydration mismatch — eltérő server/client output

topics.name: Browser Rendering and Frontend Performance
topics.description: Understanding layout, paint, long tasks, responsiveness, and real-user performance.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

The browser turns DOM and CSS into pixels through style calculation, layout, paint, and compositing. JavaScript on the main thread competes with this work.

## What problem does it solve?

Long tasks, layout thrashing, large DOM trees, and excessive script work cause input lag, dropped frames, and slow loading.

## How it works

- Use performance traces and real-user data.
- Batch DOM reads and writes.
- Use requestAnimationFrame for visual updates.
- Reduce long main-thread tasks and unused JavaScript.
- Test slow devices and networks.
- Optimize images, fonts, caching, and rendering together.

## Common mistakes

- Optimizing only bundle size.
- Testing only on a fast laptop.
- Alternating layout reads and writes in a loop.
- Memoizing before finding the bottleneck.

## Key terms

- layout — calculating geometry
- paint — recording visual output
- long task — main-thread work blocking responsiveness
- field data — measurements from real users

hu.name: Browser Rendering és Frontend Performance
hu.description: Layout, paint, long task, responsiveness és real-user performance megértése.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A browser style calculation, layout, paint és compositing során alakítja a DOM-ot és CSS-t pixellé. A main-thread JavaScript ugyanazért az időért versenyez.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Long task, layout thrashing, nagy DOM és túl sok script work input lagot, dropped frame-et és slow loadingot okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Performance trace-et és real-user data-t használj.
- DOM readet és write-ot batch-elj.
- Visual update-et requestAnimationFrame-mel igazíts frame-hez.
- Csökkentsd a long taskot és unused JavaScriptet.
- Slow device-on és networkön is tesztelj.
- Image, font, cache és rendering együtt optimalizálandó.

## Gyakori hibák

- Csak bundle size-t optimalizálni.
- Csak gyors laptopon tesztelni.
- Loopban felváltva layoutot olvasni és írni.
- Bottleneck előtt memoizálni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- layout — geometry kiszámítása
- paint — visual output elkészítése
- long task — responsiveness-t blokkoló main-thread work
- field data — real-user measurement

topics.name: Code Splitting and Lazy Loading
topics.description: Loading routes and features only when they are likely to be needed.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Code splitting creates separately loadable chunks. Lazy loading delays a route, component, image, or feature until an appropriate trigger.

## What problem does it solve?

Shipping the whole application upfront delays parsing and execution, while too many tiny chunks create waterfalls and repeated loading states.

## How it works

- Split at route or large feature boundaries.
- Preload likely next navigation when useful.
- Provide meaningful loading fallbacks.
- Coordinate code and data loading to avoid serial waterfalls.
- Track duplicated dependencies and chunk churn.

## Common mistakes

- Lazy-loading every small component.
- Creating chunks that immediately import most of the app.
- Showing a full-page spinner for every nested boundary.

## Key terms

- chunk — separately loadable bundle part
- lazy loading — delayed resource loading
- preload — loading before immediate use
- waterfall — sequential dependency chain

hu.name: Code Splitting és Lazy Loading
hu.description: Route és feature betöltése csak akkor, amikor valószínűleg szükség lesz rá.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Code splitting külön betölthető chunkokat készít. Lazy loading route, component, image vagy feature betöltését megfelelő triggerig késlelteti.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Az egész app upfront szállítása lassítja a parse és execution időt, túl sok kis chunk pedig waterfallt és ismételt loading state-et okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Route vagy nagy feature boundaryn splitelj.
- Valószínű next navigationt szükség esetén preloadolj.
- Legyen meaningful loading fallback.
- Code és data loading együtt kerülje a serial waterfallt.
- Kövesd a duplicated dependencyt és chunk churnt.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden kis componentet lazy-loadolni.
- Olyan chunkot készíteni, amely rögtön importálja az app nagy részét.
- Minden nested boundaryhez full-page spinnert adni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- chunk — külön betölthető bundle rész
- lazy loading — késleltetett resource loading
- preload — használat előtti betöltés
- waterfall — sequential dependency chain

topics.name: Accessibility and Responsive UI
topics.description: Building interfaces that work with keyboard, screen readers, zoom, long text, and varied screen sizes.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Accessible responsive UI uses semantic HTML, keyboard support, focus management, flexible layout, and content-aware sizing.

## What problem does it solve?

Mouse-only controls, fixed dimensions, colour-only messages, and desktop assumptions exclude users and break under zoom, translation, or small screens.

## How it works

- Start with semantic HTML and native controls.
- Provide labels, headings, keyboard navigation, and visible focus.
- Manage focus for dialogs and errors.
- Use ARIA only to fill native semantic gaps.
- Use flexible layout with Flexbox, Grid, and min/max constraints.
- Test zoom, long text, narrow screens, and touch targets.

## Common mistakes

- Clickable divs instead of buttons.
- Removing focus outlines.
- Relying on colour alone.
- Fixed widths based on English text.

## Key terms

- semantic HTML — elements chosen by meaning
- focus management — controlling keyboard focus
- responsive layout — adapting to available space
- accessible name — programmatic control label

hu.name: Accessibility és Responsive UI
hu.description: Keyboarddal, screen readerrel, zoommal, long texttel és eltérő screen size-zal is működő interface.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Accessible responsive UI semantic HTML-t, keyboard supportot, focus managementet, flexible layoutot és content-aware sizingot használ.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Mouse-only control, fixed dimension, colour-only message és desktop assumption kizár usert, és zoom, translation vagy small screen mellett eltörik.

## Hogyan működik?

- Semantic HTML és native control legyen az alap.
- Legyen label, heading, keyboard navigation és visible focus.
- Dialog és error esetén focus management kell.
- ARIA csak native semantic gap pótlására való.
- Flexible layout Flexbox, Grid és min/max constraint segítségével.
- Tesztelj zoomot, long textet, narrow screent és touch targetet.

## Gyakori hibák

- Clickable divet használni button helyett.
- Focus outline-t eltávolítani.
- Csak colourrel kommunikálni.
- English textre épített fixed widthöt használni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- semantic HTML — meaning alapján választott element
- focus management — keyboard focus kontrollja
- responsive layout — available space-hez alkalmazkodó layout
- accessible name — programmatic control label

topics.name: Frontend Testing and Quality Gates
topics.description: Layered automated checks protecting behaviour, accessibility, and visual consistency.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Frontend quality combines type checks, linting, unit and component tests, integration and end-to-end tests, accessibility checks, visual regression, and controlled rollout.

## What problem does it solve?

Manual testing alone is slow and usually covers only the happy path across many browser and interaction states.

## How it works

- Test pure logic cheaply.
- Test components through user-visible behaviour.
- Use integration tests for forms, routing, data, and state collaboration.
- Keep a small set of critical E2E flows.
- Add visual and accessibility checks for recurring defects.
- Fix or remove flaky tests.

## Common mistakes

- Testing implementation details.
- Replacing all tests with slow E2E.
- Keeping flaky tests teams ignore.
- Treating snapshots as meaningful coverage by default.

## Key terms

- component test — isolated UI behaviour test
- integration test — several parts working together
- visual regression — image comparison for UI changes
- quality gate — required automated release condition

hu.name: Frontend Testing és Quality Gates
hu.description: Layered automated check behaviour, accessibility és visual consistency védelmére.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Frontend quality type checket, lintet, unit és component testet, integration és E2E testet, accessibility checket, visual regressiont és controlled rolloutot kombinál.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Manual testing lassú, és sok browser vagy interaction state közül általában csak happy pathot fed le.

## Hogyan működik?

- Pure logicot olcsón tesztelj.
- Componentet user-visible behaviour alapján tesztelj.
- Form, routing, data és state collaboration integration testbe való.
- Kevés critical E2E flow maradjon.
- Recurring defecthez visual és accessibility check kell.
- Flaky testet javíts vagy törölj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Implementation detailt tesztelni.
- Minden testet slow E2E-vel helyettesíteni.
- Ignorált flaky testet megtartani.
- Snapshotot automatikusan meaningful coverage-nek tekinteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- component test — isolated UI behaviour test
- integration test — több rész együttműködésének tesztje
- visual regression — UI image comparison
- quality gate — kötelező automated release condition

topics.name: Legacy Frontend Migration
topics.description: Replacing old UI incrementally while preserving behaviour and delivery.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Legacy migration replaces old templates, jQuery, outdated React, or build tooling in small controlled steps instead of one large rewrite.

## What problem does it solve?

Big-bang rewrites pause delivery and lose hidden production behaviour.

## How it works

- Understand current behaviour and test critical flows first.
- Start with isolated, actively changing, or high-risk areas.
- Create a boundary where new and legacy code can coexist.
- Upgrade dependencies and deprecated APIs in reviewable steps.
- Keep rollback and behaviour comparison.
- Remove old paths after replacement.

## Common mistakes

- Rewriting stable code only for style.
- Migrating without a behaviour baseline.
- Keeping old and new paths forever.
- Changing framework, tooling, architecture, and product behaviour at once.

## Key terms

- strangler migration — gradual replacement
- behaviour parity — equivalent old and new outcomes
- migration seam — safe coexistence boundary
- retirement plan — explicit legacy removal

hu.name: Legacy Frontend Migration
hu.description: Régi UI fokozatos cseréje behaviour és delivery megőrzése mellett.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Legacy migration régi template-et, jQueryt, outdated Reactot vagy build toolingot kis, kontrollált stepben cserél le big-bang rewrite helyett.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A big-bang rewrite leállítja a deliveryt és elveszíti a hidden production behaviourt.

## Hogyan működik?

- Először értsd meg a behaviourt és teszteld a critical flow-t.
- Isolated, actively changing vagy high-risk területtel kezdj.
- Legyen boundary new és legacy code coexistence-hez.
- Dependency és deprecated API reviewable stepben frissüljön.
- Rollback és behaviour comparison maradjon.
- Replacement után old path törlődjön.

## Gyakori hibák

- Stable code-ot csak style miatt újraírni.
- Behaviour baseline nélkül migrálni.
- Old és new pathot örökké együtt tartani.
- Framework, tooling, architecture és product behaviour változást egyszerre csinálni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- strangler migration — gradual replacement
- behaviour parity — equivalent outcome
- migration seam — safe coexistence boundary
- retirement plan — legacy eltávolítási terv

topics.name: Frontend Localization
topics.description: Designing text, formatting, layout, and hydration for multiple locales.
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Localization includes translated text, date and number formatting, plural rules, direction, and locale-specific content.

## What problem does it solve?

Hard-coded text and fixed layouts break with long translations, right-to-left direction, and server-client locale mismatches.

## How it works

- Use stable translation keys.
- Format dates, numbers, and currencies with locale-aware APIs.
- Support pluralization and variables through the translation system.
- Test long text, missing keys, fallback locale, and RTL where relevant.
- Keep server and client locale consistent during hydration.

## Common mistakes

- Concatenating translated fragments.
- Assuming English text length.
- Storing formatted locale text as canonical data.
- Choosing different initial locales on server and client.

## Key terms

- locale — language and regional context
- translation key — stable text identifier
- plural rule — locale-specific grammatical selection
- fallback locale — locale used for missing text

hu.name: Frontend Localization
hu.description: Text, formatting, layout és hydration tervezése több locale számára.
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Localization translated textet, date és number formattingot, plural rule-t, directiont és locale-specific contentet kezel.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Hard-coded text és fixed layout eltörik long translation, RTL direction és server-client locale mismatch esetén.

## Hogyan működik?

- Stable translation key-t használj.
- Date, number és currency locale-aware API-val formázódjon.
- Pluralization és variable a translation systemen keresztül működjön.
- Tesztelj long textet, missing key-t, fallback locale-t és szükség esetén RTL-t.
- Hydration alatt server és client locale legyen azonos.

## Gyakori hibák

- Translated fragmenteket concatenálni.
- English text lengthre számítani.
- Formatted locale textet canonical data-ként tárolni.
- Serveren és clienten eltérő initial locale-t választani.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- locale — language és regional context
- translation key — stabil text identifier
- plural rule — locale-specific grammatical selection
- fallback locale — missing texthez használt locale

questions.title: Structuring a large React application
questions.prompt: A React application has grown into global components, hooks, services, and utilities folders. How would you restructure it without a risky rewrite?
questions.answerStructureHint: Define feature boundaries, keep routes thin, control shared code, and migrate in small tested steps.

expectedPoints.label: Defines feature boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Groups UI, state, API, tests, and types around product capabilities.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps routes as composition points
expectedPoints.description: Uses routes mainly to compose features rather than hold workflows.

expectedPoints.label: Controls shared code
expectedPoints.description: Moves only generic code to shared and exposes small feature APIs.

expectedPoints.label: Migrates incrementally
expectedPoints.description: Moves high-value areas first with tests and behaviour preservation.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent features from importing each other’s internals?

hu.title: Nagy React application strukturálása
hu.prompt: Egy React app globális components, hooks, services és utilities folderekre nőtt. Hogyan alakítanád át risky rewrite nélkül?
hu.answerStructureHint: Feature boundary, thin route, kontrollált shared code és tested incremental migration.

fe_struct_bound.label: Feature boundaryket definiál
fe_struct_bound.description: UI, state, API, test és type product capability köré kerül.

fe_struct_routes.label: A route composition point marad
fe_struct_routes.description: A page feature-öket rak össze, nem workflow-t tart.

fe_struct_shared.label: Kontrollálja a shared code-ot
fe_struct_shared.description: Csak generic code kerül sharedbe, kis feature public API-val.

fe_struct_migrate.label: Incremental migrationt használ
fe_struct_migrate.description: High-value területtel kezd, testtel és behaviour preservationnel.

followUps.fe_struct_fu: Hogyan akadályoznád meg a feature internalok keresztimportját?

questions.title: A React page re-renders too much
questions.prompt: A page feels slow and many components re-render. Walk through how you would investigate and optimize it.
questions.answerStructureHint: Measure first, find the trigger, apply structural fixes, then targeted memoization or virtualization.

expectedPoints.label: Measures first
expectedPoints.description: Uses React Profiler or browser tooling to locate expensive work.

expectedPoints.label: Finds the trigger
expectedPoints.description: Checks state scope, parent renders, Context, selectors, and unstable props.

expectedPoints.label: Applies structural fixes
expectedPoints.description: Localizes state, splits components, and narrows subscriptions.

expectedPoints.label: Uses targeted optimization
expectedPoints.description: Uses memo, useMemo, useCallback, or virtualization for a measured cause.

followUps.prompt: Why can React.memo fail when the parent passes an inline object or function?

hu.title: Túl sok React re-render vizsgálata
hu.prompt: Egy page lassú, és sok component re-renderel. Hogyan vizsgálnád és optimalizálnád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Measurement, trigger, structural fix, majd targeted memoization vagy virtualization.

react_perf_measure.label: Először mér
react_perf_measure.description: React Profiler vagy browser tool alapján keresi az expensive worköt.

react_perf_trigger.label: Megkeresi a triggert
react_perf_trigger.description: State scope, parent render, Context, selector és unstable props kerül elő.

react_perf_structure.label: Strukturális javítást alkalmaz
react_perf_structure.description: State localization, component split és narrow subscription szerepel.

react_perf_target.label: Targeted optimizationt használ
react_perf_target.description: memo, useMemo, useCallback vagy virtualization csak mért okra kerül be.

followUps.react_perf_fu: Miért nem segít a React.memo, ha a parent inline objectet vagy functiont ad?

questions.title: List state moves to the wrong row
questions.prompt: After inserting an item at the top of a list, input values and focus appear in the wrong rows. What is the likely cause and fix?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain positional identity, stable keys, and why random keys are also wrong.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies unstable keys
expectedPoints.description: Recognizes array indexes or changing keys as the cause.

expectedPoints.label: Explains identity
expectedPoints.description: Explains that React associates state and DOM with type and key.

expectedPoints.label: Uses stable IDs
expectedPoints.description: Uses an identifier belonging to the logical item.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids random keys
expectedPoints.description: Notes that random keys remount rows and lose state.

followUps.prompt: When would deliberately changing a key be useful?

hu.title: A list state rossz sorra kerül
hu.prompt: Egy item beszúrása után az input value és focus rossz sorokban jelenik meg. Mi az ok és javítás?
hu.answerStructureHint: Positional identity, stable key és random key problémája.

react_key_cause.label: Felismeri az unstable key-t
react_key_cause.description: Array index vagy változó key okozza az identity problémát.

react_key_identity.label: Elmagyarázza az identityt
react_key_identity.description: React type és key alapján társít state-et és DOM-ot.

react_key_fix.label: Stabil ID-t használ
react_key_fix.description: A logikai item persistent identifierje lesz a key.

react_key_random.label: Elkerüli a random key-t
react_key_random.description: Random key remountot és state loss-t okoz.

followUps.react_key_fu: Mikor lehet hasznos a key szándékos megváltoztatása?

questions.title: Everything is stored globally
questions.prompt: Modal state, API responses, filters, user session, and form fields all live in one store. What stays and what moves?
questions.answerStructureHint: Classify local, shared, server, and navigation state, then place each by lifecycle.

expectedPoints.label: Classifies state
expectedPoints.description: Separates local UI, shared client, server, and navigation state.

expectedPoints.label: Moves local state closer
expectedPoints.description: Keeps modal and isolated form state near the owning UI.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a query cache
expectedPoints.description: Uses server-state tooling for caching, freshness, refetch, and invalidation.

expectedPoints.label: Uses URL state
expectedPoints.description: Places shareable filters and pagination in the URL.

followUps.prompt: Where would Context fit after this cleanup?

hu.title: Minden state global store-ban van
hu.prompt: Modal, API response, filter, user session és form field is egy store-ban él. Mi maradna és mit mozgatnál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Local, shared, server és navigation state osztályozása lifecycle alapján.

react_state_class.label: Osztályozza a state-et
react_state_class.description: Local UI, shared client, server és navigation state külön kategória.

react_state_local.label: Local state-et közelebb viszi
react_state_local.description: Modal és isolated form state a használó UI közelébe kerül.

react_state_server.label: Query cache-t használ
react_state_server.description: Caching, freshness, refetch és invalidation server-state tool feladata.

react_state_url.label: URL state-et használ
react_state_url.description: Shareable filter és pagination URL-ben él.

followUps.react_state_fu: Hol lenne helye a Contextnek a cleanup után?

questions.title: A Context causes broad re-renders
questions.prompt: One Context contains user data, notifications, live counters, and actions. Almost every change re-renders most of the app. How would you improve it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Split by domain and update frequency, control value identity, and choose granular subscriptions where needed.

expectedPoints.label: Splits Contexts
expectedPoints.description: Separates unrelated domains and frequently changing values.

expectedPoints.label: Controls provider identity
expectedPoints.description: Avoids unnecessary fresh compound values and functions.

expectedPoints.label: Uses another model where needed
expectedPoints.description: Keeps high-frequency state local or uses selector-based subscriptions.

expectedPoints.label: Profiles consumers
expectedPoints.description: Measures which updates and consumers are expensive.

followUps.prompt: Why might separating state and dispatch Contexts help?

hu.title: Egy Context széles re-rendert okoz
hu.prompt: Egy Context user data-t, notificationt, live countert és actiont tartalmaz. Szinte minden change az app nagy részét re-rendereli. Mit tennél?
hu.answerStructureHint: Domain/update frequency split, value identity és granular subscription.

react_ctx_split.label: Felosztja a Contextet
react_ctx_split.description: Unrelated domain és gyakran változó value külön providerbe kerül.

react_ctx_identity.label: Kontrollálja a provider identityt
react_ctx_identity.description: Indokolatlan fresh compound value és function megszűnik.

react_ctx_alt.label: Más modellt választ, ha kell
react_ctx_alt.description: High-frequency state local vagy selectoros subscription lesz.

react_ctx_measure.label: Profilozza a consumereket
react_ctx_measure.description: Mérés alapján derül ki, mely update expensive.

followUps.react_ctx_fu: Miért segíthet a state és dispatch Context különválasztása?

questions.title: When Redux-Saga is justified
questions.prompt: A team proposes Redux-Saga for every API call. Which workflows justify it, and when would you choose a simpler tool?
questions.answerStructureHint: Separate orchestration from fetching, then discuss cancellation, sequencing, and cache needs.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies orchestration cases
expectedPoints.description: Uses Saga for cross-module sequences, cancellation, races, or background workflows.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids Saga for simple fetching
expectedPoints.description: Uses a query library when the need is cache, refetch, and invalidation.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps effects declarative
expectedPoints.description: Uses generator effects and keeps reducers pure.

expectedPoints.label: Models obsolete work
expectedPoints.description: Uses takeLatest or explicit cancellation where appropriate.

followUps.prompt: Give a case where takeEvery is safer than takeLatest.

hu.title: Mikor indokolt a Redux-Saga?
hu.prompt: A team minden API callhoz Redux-Sagát javasol. Mely workflow-k indokolják, és mikor választanál egyszerűbb toolt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Orchestration és fetching különválasztása, cancellation, sequencing és cache need.

react_saga_orch.label: Felismeri az orchestration esetet
react_saga_orch.description: Cross-module sequence, cancellation, race vagy background workflow indokolhat Sagát.

react_saga_simple.label: Simple fetchhez nem használ Sagát
react_saga_simple.description: Cache, refetch és invalidation problémára query libraryt választ.

react_saga_effect.label: Declarative effectet használ
react_saga_effect.description: Generator effectekkel dolgozik, a reducer pure marad.

react_saga_stale.label: Kezeli az obsolete worköt
react_saga_stale.description: takeLatest vagy explicit cancellation kerül elő.

followUps.react_saga_fu: Mondj esetet, amikor a takeEvery biztonságosabb, mint a takeLatest.

questions.title: An effect creates duplicate subscriptions
questions.prompt: In development, a component subscribes twice and receives duplicate events. The team wants to disable StrictMode. What is the better response?
questions.answerStructureHint: Treat repeated setup as a test, make setup safe, add cleanup, and verify dependencies.

expectedPoints.label: Understands the development check
expectedPoints.description: Explains that repeated setup exposes unsafe effects rather than normal production duplication.

expectedPoints.label: Adds cleanup
expectedPoints.description: Unsubscribes or destroys the external resource before rerun and unmount.

expectedPoints.label: Checks dependencies
expectedPoints.description: Includes reactive inputs and avoids unstable accidental resubscription.

expectedPoints.label: Makes setup safe
expectedPoints.description: Ensures repeated setup and cleanup leave the external system consistent.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent an older fetch response from updating after parameters change?

hu.title: Egy effect duplicate subscriptiont hoz létre
hu.prompt: Developmentben egy component kétszer subscribe-ol. A team kikapcsolná a StrictMode-ot. Mi a jobb megoldás?
hu.answerStructureHint: Repeated setup mint test, safe setup, cleanup és helyes dependency.

react_eff_check.label: Érti a development checket
react_eff_check.description: A repeated setup unsafe effectet tár fel, nem normál production duplication.

react_eff_cleanup.label: Cleanupot ad
react_eff_cleanup.description: Rerun és unmount előtt unsubscribe vagy resource destroy történik.

react_eff_deps.label: Ellenőrzi a dependencyket
react_eff_deps.description: Reactive input szerepel, unstable dependency nem okoz accidental resubscriptiont.

react_eff_safe.label: Safe setupot készít
react_eff_safe.description: Repeated setup és cleanup után az external system konzisztens marad.

followUps.react_eff_fu: Hogyan akadályoznád meg, hogy parameter change után régebbi fetch response update-eljen?

questions.title: Designing a robust form
questions.prompt: A form loses user input after a server error and reports every validation failure in one banner. How would you redesign it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Preserve values, associate errors with fields, model submission states, and keep one ownership model.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves input
expectedPoints.description: Keeps entered values after recoverable validation or server failures.

expectedPoints.label: Associates field errors
expectedPoints.description: Maps structured errors to labelled fields and useful focus behaviour.

expectedPoints.label: Models submission states
expectedPoints.description: Separates idle, submitting, success, validation failure, and server failure.

expectedPoints.label: Uses consistent ownership
expectedPoints.description: Chooses controlled, uncontrolled, or a form library deliberately.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent accidental double submission?

hu.title: Robusztus form tervezése
hu.prompt: Egy form server error után elveszíti a user inputot, és minden validation failure-t egy bannerben mutat. Hogyan terveznéd újra?
hu.answerStructureHint: Value preservation, field error association, submission state és egyértelmű ownership.

react_form_keep.label: Megőrzi a user inputot
react_form_keep.description: Recoverable failure után a beírt value megmarad.

react_form_field.label: Fieldhez kapcsolja az errort
react_form_field.description: Structured error a megfelelő labelled fieldhez és focus behaviourhöz kapcsolódik.

react_form_states.label: Modellezi a submission state-eket
react_form_states.description: Idle, submitting, success, validation failure és server failure külön állapot.

react_form_owner.label: Konzisztens ownershipot használ
react_form_owner.description: Controlled, uncontrolled vagy form library tudatos választás.

followUps.react_form_fu: Hogyan akadályoznád meg az accidental double submissiont?

questions.title: The design contains only the happy path
questions.prompt: You receive a list-screen design that only shows successful data. What additional states and backend contract details do you need?
questions.answerStructureHint: Cover loading, empty, error categories, recovery, stale requests, and machine-readable errors.

expectedPoints.label: Names core states
expectedPoints.description: Designs loading, empty, error, and success explicitly.

expectedPoints.label: Separates error categories
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes validation, permission, not-found, network, and server failures.

expectedPoints.label: Requests a structured contract
expectedPoints.description: Needs stable status and machine-readable error bodies.

expectedPoints.label: Handles timing and races
expectedPoints.description: Avoids flashing loaders and prevents stale responses from overwriting newer results.

followUps.prompt: Which actions would be suitable for optimistic UI?

hu.title: A design csak happy pathot tartalmaz
hu.prompt: Egy list screen design csak successful data-t mutat. Milyen state és backend contract detail kell még?
hu.answerStructureHint: Loading, empty, error category, recovery, stale request és machine-readable error.

fe_states_core.label: Megnevezi a core state-eket
fe_states_core.description: Loading, empty, error és success explicit design része.

fe_states_errors.label: Elkülöníti az error categoryket
fe_states_errors.description: Validation, permission, not-found, network és server failure külön kezelést kap.

fe_states_contract.label: Structured contractot kér
fe_states_contract.description: Stabil status és machine-readable error body alapján választ UI-t.

fe_states_race.label: Kezeli a timingot és race-t
fe_states_race.description: Nem villog a loader, és stale response nem írja felül az új resultot.

followUps.fe_states_fu: Mely action lenne alkalmas optimistic UI-ra?

questions.title: Debugging a hydration mismatch
questions.prompt: An SSR page reports hydration mismatches only for some users and locales. How would you investigate it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Compare server and first client output, inspect nondeterministic inputs, and make the first render consistent.

expectedPoints.label: Compares output
expectedPoints.description: Finds the exact node and value that differs in the first render.

expectedPoints.label: Checks common sources
expectedPoints.description: Investigates time, random values, locale formatting, browser APIs, and local storage.

expectedPoints.label: Makes rendering deterministic
expectedPoints.description: Uses server-provided initial data or moves client-only differences after hydration.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids blanket suppression
expectedPoints.description: Fixes the cause instead of hiding all warnings.

followUps.prompt: Why can server HTML be visible before the page is fully interactive?

hu.title: Hydration mismatch hibakeresése
hu.prompt: Egy SSR page csak bizonyos usereknél és locale-oknál jelez hydration mismatch-et. Hogyan vizsgálnád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Server és first client output, nondeterministic input és deterministic first render.

fe_hyd_compare.label: Összehasonlítja az outputot
fe_hyd_compare.description: Megkeresi a first render pontosan eltérő node-ját és value-ját.

fe_hyd_sources.label: Vizsgálja a tipikus forrásokat
fe_hyd_sources.description: Time, random value, locale formatting, browser API és localStorage kerül elő.

fe_hyd_fix.label: Deterministic rendert készít
fe_hyd_fix.description: Server-provided initial data vagy hydration utáni client-only difference lesz.

fe_hyd_nohide.label: Nem rejt el minden warningot
fe_hyd_nohide.description: Suppression helyett root cause fix történik.

followUps.fe_hyd_fu: Miért lehet a server HTML látható azelőtt, hogy a page fully interactive lenne?

questions.title: Rendering one million rows
questions.prompt: A product owner asks to render one million rows in a React table. How would you design the experience and implementation?
questions.answerStructureHint: Challenge the requirement, limit delivered data, virtualize visible rows, and design complete UX.

expectedPoints.label: Challenges the raw requirement
expectedPoints.description: Asks what users need to search, compare, export, or act on.

expectedPoints.label: Limits delivered data
expectedPoints.description: Uses server filtering, pagination, cursoring, or incremental loading.

expectedPoints.label: Virtualizes rows
expectedPoints.description: Mounts only the viewport and overscan area.

expectedPoints.label: Designs complete UX
expectedPoints.description: Handles loading, keyboard, focus, empty results, and row-size constraints.

followUps.prompt: What complications appear when row heights are dynamic?

hu.title: Egymillió sor renderelése
hu.prompt: A product owner egymillió sort akar egy React table-ben. Hogyan terveznéd az experience-et és implementationt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Requirement challenge, kevesebb data, virtualization és complete UX.

fe_list_need.label: Megkérdőjelezi a requirementet
fe_list_need.description: Tisztázza, mit keres, hasonlít, exportál vagy módosít a user.

fe_list_data.label: Korlátozza a delivered data-t
fe_list_data.description: Server filtering, pagination, cursor vagy incremental loading szerepel.

fe_list_ux.label: Teljes UX-et tervez
fe_list_ux.description: Loading, keyboard, focus, empty result és row-size constraint is kezelt.

followUps.fe_list_fu: Milyen plusz problémát okoz a dynamic row height?

questions.title: Reviewing a Figma handoff
questions.prompt: A designer provides desktop happy-path screens for a new feature. What would you clarify before estimating and implementing it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Find missing states, responsive and content behaviour, accessibility interactions, and data assumptions.

expectedPoints.label: Finds missing states
expectedPoints.description: Asks about loading, empty, error, disabled, permission, and partial data.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies responsive content
expectedPoints.description: Checks small screens, long translations, wrapping, overflow, and dynamic content.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies accessibility
expectedPoints.description: Covers keyboard, focus, labels, modal behaviour, and screen-reader semantics.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies data behaviour
expectedPoints.description: Asks about API states, validation, latency, retry, and optimistic behaviour.

followUps.prompt: How would you decide whether part of the design belongs in the shared design system?

hu.title: Figma handoff felülvizsgálata
hu.prompt: A designer csak desktop happy-path screeneket ad. Mit tisztáznál estimation és implementation előtt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Missing state, responsive content, accessibility és data behaviour.

fe_figma_states.label: Megkeresi a missing state-eket
fe_figma_states.description: Loading, empty, error, disabled, permission és partial data tisztázott.

fe_figma_resp.label: Tisztázza a responsive contentet
fe_figma_resp.description: Small screen, long translation, wrapping, overflow és dynamic content szerepel.

fe_figma_access.label: Tisztázza az accessibilityt
fe_figma_access.description: Keyboard, focus, label, modal és screen-reader semantics kerül elő.

fe_figma_data.label: Tisztázza a data behaviourt
fe_figma_data.description: API state, validation, latency, retry és optimistic behaviour szerepel.

followUps.fe_figma_fu: Hogyan döntöd el, hogy valami a shared design systembe tartozik-e?

questions.title: Do we need microfrontends?
questions.prompt: Several teams want independent frontend deployment. How would you decide between a modular frontend and microfrontends?
questions.answerStructureHint: Start with organisation and deployment pain, compare complexity, and define UX governance.

expectedPoints.label: Requires a real reason
expectedPoints.description: Looks for team autonomy, deployment coupling, ownership, or migration.

expectedPoints.label: Considers modular first
expectedPoints.description: Uses strong internal boundaries when one deployment is sufficient.

expectedPoints.label: Names costs
expectedPoints.description: Covers runtime integration, dependency versions, routing, auth, observability, and debugging.

expectedPoints.label: Defines UX governance
expectedPoints.description: Uses a shared design system and minimal contract-based communication.

followUps.prompt: Why is one shared global Redux store a warning sign across microfrontends?

hu.title: Szükség van microfrontendre?
hu.prompt: Több team independent frontend deploymentet szeretne. Hogyan döntenél modular frontend és microfrontends között?
hu.answerStructureHint: Organisation és deployment pain, complexity és UX governance.

fe_mf_reason.label: Valódi indokot kér
fe_mf_reason.description: Team autonomy, deployment coupling, ownership vagy migration indokol.

fe_mf_modular.label: Először modular frontendet mérlegel
fe_mf_modular.description: Egy deployment mellett strong internal boundary egyszerűbb.

fe_mf_cost.label: Megnevezi a costot
fe_mf_cost.description: Runtime integration, dependency, routing, auth, observability és debugging szerepel.

fe_mf_ux.label: UX governance-t definiál
fe_mf_ux.description: Közös design system és minimal contract-based communication kell.

followUps.fe_mf_fu: Miért warning sign egy közös global Redux store microfrontendek között?

questions.title: Migrating a legacy frontend safely
questions.prompt: A business wants to replace a jQuery and server-template frontend with React. How would you plan the migration and quality gates?
questions.answerStructureHint: Build a behaviour baseline, create migration seams, replace isolated areas, and automate regression checks.

expectedPoints.label: Builds a baseline
expectedPoints.description: Documents and tests critical existing flows first.

expectedPoints.label: Migrates incrementally
expectedPoints.description: Starts with isolated, high-change, or high-value areas.

expectedPoints.label: Creates coexistence boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Defines temporary routing, DOM ownership, data, and event integration.

expectedPoints.label: Adds layered quality gates
expectedPoints.description: Uses type checks, component/integration tests, critical E2E, and visual/accessibility checks.

followUps.prompt: Would you automatically rewrite every class component to Hooks?

hu.title: Legacy frontend safe migrációja
hu.prompt: A business jQuery és server-template frontendet akar Reactre cserélni. Hogyan terveznéd a migrationt és quality gate-eket?
hu.answerStructureHint: Behaviour baseline, migration seam, isolated replacement és automated regression check.

fe_leg_baseline.label: Baseline-t készít
fe_leg_baseline.description: Critical existing flow dokumentált és tesztelt a change előtt.

fe_leg_increment.label: Incremental migrationt használ
fe_leg_increment.description: Isolated, high-change vagy high-value területtel kezd.

fe_leg_coexist.label: Coexistence boundaryt készít
fe_leg_coexist.description: Temporary routing, DOM ownership, data és event integration tisztázott.

fe_leg_quality.label: Layered quality gate-et ad
fe_leg_quality.description: Type check, component/integration test, critical E2E és visual/accessibility check szerepel.

followUps.fe_leg_fu: Automatikusan minden class componentet Hookra írnál?
